content: Semantic priming effects in human cognition can be modeled as an implicit soft Q-learning process, where prior semantic context warps the reward landscape of lexical access to favor faster retrieval of semantically related concepts, just as temperature-controlled entropy regularization in soft Q-learning encourages prior-informed stochastic exploration of action spaces. This framework generates a testable hypothesis that individual differences in semantic priming magnitude will correlate with implicit exploration temperature in reward-based decision-making tasks.
